title: Secret Killer Leads Cops Into Her $3,000,000 Murder Mansion

beats.description: Cold open with police detecting decomposition odor and narrator teasing the hidden body and meticulous cover-up.
Introduction to Craig and Dena's lifestyle, property, and the initial welfare check request from a concerned friend.
Second call from grandson reveals 4-month timeline and conflicting stories; police canvas neighbors and receive Dena's Mexico/divorce claim.
Detective investigates Dena's violent past, ex-husbands' accounts, daughter's allegations, and suspicious financial spending.
Sponsor segment bridging the background investigation to the next field interview.
Deputy Roger interviews Dena; she presents fake notes, claims ignorance of safe combination, and Roger subtly reveals his homicide background.
Roger interviews neighbors who contradict Dena's safe claims and recall her past violent threats; Dena is seen digging/flattening dirt outside.
Explosive tip from Dena's former coworker alleging she confessed to shooting Craig; Dena attempts suicide in Texas, prompting a search warrant.
Police execute search warrant, discovering bleach, disabled cameras, blood traces, and opening the safe to find only $84,000 instead of $1.5M.
Daughter Leslie calls with Dena's full confession and precise location of the body hidden under the carport.
Body recovered and identified, Dena arrested at hospital, invokes right to counsel, and case status is outlined.

_analysis_metadata.transcript_preview: Doesn't look like anyone's been here. >> What's that smell? >> This officer is unknowingly reacting to the decomposition odor coming from Craig Thatford's hidden body. Police have been searching for him for months with no success. And even now, with the foul odor in the air, they still have no idea he's buried only feet away. that dirt. Where's that blood? And >> this is the only way I've been able to have any communication with you. And I don't even know if it's him. >> I wouldn't even know where to start. Look, >> there's some weird going on. Whoever killed Craig covered their tracks meticulously using chemicals to mask the odor, disabling security cameras, and hiding in plain sight. And while ...
